title: Baked macaroni cheese
subtitle: Macaroni in a sharp Cheddar béchamel, baked with a crisp breadcrumb crust
excerpt: Macaroni in a well-seasoned Cheddar béchamel, topped with breadcrumbs and extra Cheddar and baked until the sauce bubbles and the crust is deep golden. The American baked version, distinct from the stovetop variety, with a crust that provides contrast to the soft, cheesy interior.
sourceNotes: Baked macaroni and cheese is documented in American cookery literature from the early nineteenth century. A version appears in Mary Randolph's The Virginia Housewife (1824, US public domain), described as macaroni baked with butter and cheese. The dish became a national American staple through the twentieth century.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze before baking. Defrost overnight and bake from cold, adding 15 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles well in a larger dish.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble up to 1 day ahead, cover, and refrigerate. Bake from cold, adding 10 to 15 minutes.

content.text: The béchamel wants to be generously seasoned before the cheese goes in — it needs more salt and pepper than you think, because the macaroni will absorb and dilute the seasoning as it bakes. The mustard powder and cayenne are not optional; they sharpen the cheese flavour and give the sauce the slight tang that distinguishes a good macaroni cheese from a mild one.

Undercook the macaroni by 2 minutes before adding it to the sauce — it will finish cooking in the oven and absorb liquid as it bakes. A fully cooked macaroni will turn mushy by the time the top is golden. The breadcrumb crust is worth doing properly: mix the crumbs with a little melted butter before scattering them over the top.
Cook the {{pasta-dried}} of macaroni in well-salted boiling water for 2 minutes less than the packet instructions. Drain and set aside.

Melt the sauce {{unsalted-butter}} of butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the {{plain-flour}} of flour and stir for 2 minutes to cook the raw flour taste out — the mixture should look sandy and begin to smell nutty. Gradually whisk in the warm {{whole-milk}} of milk a ladleful at a time, whisking after each addition to keep the sauce smooth. Bring to the boil, stirring, then reduce the heat and simmer for 3 minutes until thickened. Remove from the heat. Stir in the sauce {{cheddar}} of Cheddar, {{mustard-powder}} of mustard powder, and a pinch of {{cayenne}} of cayenne. Season generously with salt and pepper.

Heat the oven to 200°C (fan 180°C). Combine the drained macaroni with the cheese sauce and pour into a large baking dish. Mix the {{breadcrumbs-dried}} of breadcrumbs with the crust {{unsalted-butter}} of melted butter and the crust {{cheddar}} of grated Cheddar. Scatter evenly over the top. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until the sauce is bubbling and the crust is deep golden. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

items.symptom: Sauce has split and looks greasy
items.cause: Overheated after adding the cheese, or sauce too thick when cheese was added
items.fix: Add the cheese off the heat, not over a boiling sauce; stir gently rather than whisking hard once the cheese goes in
Always undercook the pasta by at least 2 minutes; it absorbs liquid and finishes cooking in the oven

items.cause: Not enough butter in the crumbs, or oven not hot enough
items.fix: Mix the breadcrumbs with melted butter before topping; ensure the oven is fully preheated; switch to the grill for the final 3 minutes if needed

content.text: Baked macaroni and cheese appears in American cookery literature as early as 1824 in Mary Randolph's The Virginia Housewife, suggesting it was already an established household dish by that point. The version with a breadcrumb crust and baked in the oven is the traditional American form; the stovetop version with a processed cheese sauce is a twentieth-century development. The dish became closely associated with African American Southern cooking through the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, particularly as a centrepiece of Sunday meals and holiday tables.
